title: Managed ESPM (Endpoint Security Posture Management) — early access
summary: Huntress launched Managed Endpoint Security Posture Management, designed to catch misconfigured Windows endpoints before they're exploited. Available in early access; GA targeted for summer 2026.
why_it_matters: Misconfiguration causes more breaches than zero-days. ESPM continuously checks endpoint posture (Defender state, BitLocker, firewall, secure boot) and flags drift — the kind of low-noise audit that's hard to staff in-house.

title: Managed ISPM (Identity Security Posture Management) — early access
summary: Companion to ESPM that focuses on identity-side misconfiguration: dormant admin accounts, MFA gaps, conditional-access drift, risky third-party app permissions in Microsoft 365.
why_it_matters: Identity is the new perimeter — most breaches now start with stolen credentials. ISPM finds the soft spots before attackers do.

title: 10 million Microsoft 365 identities now protected by Huntress ITDR
summary: Huntress crossed 10M+ M365 identities under ITDR coverage and announced ITDR expansion to Google Workspace.
why_it_matters: Scale is a leading indicator of detection quality — the more identities Huntress watches, the better its baseline understanding of normal vs. malicious sign-in behavior.
